implemented: The closed R0-R7 policy and JSON Schema define exact deletion eligibility, age floors, purge priority, protected classes, file-only deletion, symlink refusal, lease protection, hash recheck, and dry-run default.
Required reservation bytes use exact integer ceil(max(profile estimate, measured profile p95) * 5 / 4).
SQLite schema v4 durably records reservations, retention artifacts, immutable plan items, plans, outcomes, and hash-linked events.
Jobs cannot lease without an active storage reservation; lease acquisition consumes it and terminal completion releases it with a timestamp.
Capacity decisions map healthy/soft/hard/emergency floors to none/pause/drain/stop at 150/100/60 GiB boundaries and refuse a reservation whose projected free space crosses the soft floor.
Retention planning is deterministic and excludes R0/R1/R2/R7, protected references, active leases, young artifacts, unsafe paths, size drift, and hash drift.

Retention apply defaults to dry-run, marks exact immutable plan items, reloads mutable guards under BEGIN IMMEDIATE immediately before unlink, rechecks exact path/size/hash, and records applied or partial outcomes without promoting artifacts.
JSON CLI commands implement `maskfactory daz retention plan` and `maskfactory daz retention apply`; plan persistence and deletion are explicit opt-ins.

validation.focused_command: python -m pytest -q tests/test_daz_storage.py tests/test_daz_scheduler.py tests/test_daz_control_plane.py tests/test_daz_runtime_worker.py
validation.aggregate_note: A combined Windows pytest invocation emitted 43 progress dots and left its exact owned pytest process alive without a terminal summary; that PID alone was stopped. The affected suites were then verified by bounded independent commands, so no aggregate result is claimed from the interrupted invocation.

behaviors_proven: exact fill thresholds issue pause, drain, and controlled-stop transitions without launching a worker
a job without a reservation remains pending and cannot lease
reservation allocation is durable and projected-floor refusal is fail-closed
terminal lease completion releases the consumed reservation
two identical retention dry-runs produce the same ordered plan and plan hash
R0 and protected-reference artifacts are never selected
leases present during planning and leases acquired after initial verification prevent deletion
dry-run preserves files and explicit apply deletes only exact planned files
hash drift, paths outside the governed root, and immutable-plan mutation fail closed
remaining_acceptance: Populate governed per-profile measured p95 inputs from D9-08 rather than fixture values.
Run a bounded production F-drive fill/drain rehearsal when the dependent queue and capacity prerequisites permit it.
Run a live retention plan and explicit apply against disposable eligible F-drive artifacts, proving inventory reconciliation and rollback/recovery evidence.
Complete the dependent backup, restore, recovery, and soak items before production activation.
claim_boundary: This evidence proves the inactive reservation/retention implementation and bounded fixture behavior. It does not mutate the live F-drive queue, delete live DAZ artifacts, supply measured D9-08 profile p95 values, complete MF-P9-12.02, activate DAZ, or satisfy backup/recovery/soak gates.
